title: The Midnight Clock
summary: A man buys a special old thing from a shop.
coverScene: A young man sitting on his bed stares at an old wooden clock next to a window, while a dark shadow stands outside in the moonlit garden.

sentences.text: Leo had always been fascinated by old objects that carried a deep sense of mystery and history.
One cold afternoon, while wandering through the quiet streets of a historic town, he discovered a small, dirty shop.
Inside, the air was thick with the smell of old paper, wooden furniture, and ancient books.
His eyes were immediately drawn to a beautiful clock, which was displayed on a dark shelf in the corner.
If he had known the dark secret of this object, he would have left the shop immediately.
But he did not know, and his interest was far too strong.
The shop owner, an elderly man with pale skin and a worried expression, did not ask for much money.
In fact, he seemed almost too happy to sell the beautiful clock to the young man.
After a very brief conversation, the purchase was completed, and Leo carried his new object to his apartment.
He decided to place it on the wooden table next to his bed, where he could watch it.
As the sun went down, the room became unusually cold, and a strange silence fell over the entire house.
Leo lay in his bed, listening to the steady, comforting sound of the machine.
At midnight, Leo was suddenly woken by a loud, heavy sound as the clock began to strike twelve times.
He sat up in bed and watched the gold hands move through the darkness of his room.
To his surprise, after the twelfth strike, the long hand did not continue its normal path forward.
Instead, it made a sharp, strange sound and jumped backward to eleven, then slowly to ten.
Leo stared in complete shock as the clock began to tick backward, its sound growing louder with every second.
With every backward movement of the hand, he felt a sudden, freezing drop in the temperature of the room.
While he was watching the strange device, he noticed something unusual through his window, which looked out over the garden.
Under the pale light of the moon, a tall, dark shadow was standing near the edge of the property.
Every time the clock made a backward tick, the shadow took one step closer to his house.
When the hand reached nine, the figure was already standing near the old, dead tree.
By the time the clock ticked down to six, the shadow had reached the middle of the garden.
Leo tried to move, but his body felt completely frozen by a great sense of fear.
Finally, the clock reached one, and a loud, sharp ring sounded through the silent walls of the apartment.
In that exact moment, the shadow disappeared from the grass, leaving the garden completely empty and still.
Leo ran to the window and stared into the darkness, but there was nothing to be seen.
He did not sleep for the rest of the night, keeping his eyes on the empty garden.
When the morning light finally appeared, he tried to convince himself that it had all been a dream.
However, the deep cold in his bones remained, and he knew he had to act.
During the day, Leo decided that he could not keep the dangerous clock in his home any longer.
He carried the heavy wooden box to a bridge over the deep river at the edge of town.
With a strong push, he threw the clock into the cold water, watching it sink quickly below the surface.
He felt a wave of relief as he walked back to his apartment, believing his trouble was over.
But when he opened his bedroom door, his heart stopped in shock and fear.
The beautiful clock was sitting on his table, completely dry and ticking as if nothing had happened.
As the sun set once more, Leo realized that he was trapped in a terrible situation.
Night fell, and he sat on his bed, too afraid to turn off the lights or close his eyes.
When midnight arrived, the clock struck twelve and immediately began its backward movement toward one.
Leo ran to the window and looked out into the garden with growing fear.
The shadow was there, but it did not start at the far fence this time.
It was standing near the middle of the garden, exactly where it had disappeared the previous night.
With the first backward tick, the shadow took a step toward the house, its dark form growing larger.
By the time the hand reached ten, the figure was standing on the stone path just outside.
Leo wanted to scream, but no sound could escape his dry throat as he watched the creature approach.
At seven, the shadow was standing directly beneath his window, its face hidden in the deep darkness.
If he had owned a weapon, he would have tried to defend himself, but he was completely weak.
He could only watch in terror as the hand continued to move backward.
The clock ticked to four, and a cold hand seemed to touch the glass of his window.
At three, the glass began to crack under a terrible, silent pressure that filled the room.
When the hand reached two, the window flew open, and a freezing wind rushed inside, blowing the curtains.
The shadow was now standing inside his room, its cold presence filling every corner with absolute darkness.
As the clock prepared to make its final tick to one, Leo closed his eyes and waited.
If only he had never entered that small, dirty shop in the historic town.

quiz.q: What does the clock do every night at midnight?
options: It stops ticking until the morning.
It strikes thirteen times instead of twelve.
It ticks backward from twelve to one.

quiz.q: What does Leo do to try and get rid of the clock?
options: He throws it into a deep river.
He sells it back to the old shop owner.
He locks it inside a wooden box in his basement.

quiz.q: What happens on the second night when the clock ticks backward?
options: The shadow starts moving from the far fence again.
The shadow starts from the middle of the garden where it had disappeared.
The shadow does not appear at all because of the rain.

What does Leo do with the clock during the day?

He throws it into a river.

quiz.q: Where is the dark figure when it disappears on the first night?

Leo took the object to his home.
He put it on a table near his bed.
The sun went down.

Leo lay in bed and listened to the clock.
At midnight, the clock struck twelve times.
The sound was heavy.

It moved to eleven, then ten.
The clock was moving back.

At six, it was in the middle of the garden.
Leo was afraid and could not move.
Finally, the clock reached one.
A ring sounded through the room.

Leo ran to the window.
He saw nothing in the dark.

But he still felt very cold.
During the day, Leo decided to act.
He could not keep the bad clock.
He carried the heavy box to a road over the water.
He threw the clock into the deep river.

He felt happy and went home.

It had no water on it.
It was moving.

It began to move back again.

He was sorry he entered that shop.
